template: Summarize this customer review: '{{reviewText}}'

inputs.reviewText: The battery life is great but the screen is dim in sunlight.

testData.expectedOutput: The product excels in battery life but struggles with screen visibility outdoors.
testData.actualOutput: The product has strong battery performance but poor screen visibility in bright outdoor conditions.

inputs.reviewText: Incredibly fast delivery but the packaging was damaged on arrival.

testData.expectedOutput: Delivery speed was excellent but the packaging arrived in poor condition.
testData.actualOutput: Fast shipping noted, but the item arrived with damaged packaging.

inputs.reviewText: The app is intuitive but crashes frequently on older devices.

testData.expectedOutput: The app is easy to use but has stability issues on older hardware.
testData.actualOutput: App usability is strong but reliability on older devices is a concern.

template: You are a product feedback analyst. Summarize the following customer review in one sentence, highlighting sentiment for each mentioned feature. Review: '{{reviewText}}'

inputs.reviewText: The battery life is great but the screen is dim in sunlight.

testData.expectedOutput: The product excels in battery life but struggles with screen visibility outdoors.
testData.actualOutput: Battery life received positive sentiment while screen brightness in sunlight was flagged as a limitation.

inputs.reviewText: Incredibly fast delivery but the packaging was damaged on arrival.

testData.expectedOutput: Delivery speed was excellent but the packaging arrived in poor condition.
testData.actualOutput: Delivery speed received strong positive sentiment while packaging condition on arrival was flagged as a failure.

inputs.reviewText: The app is intuitive but crashes frequently on older devices.

testData.expectedOutput: The app is easy to use but has stability issues on older hardware.
testData.actualOutput: App intuitiveness received positive sentiment while frequent crashes on older devices were flagged as a critical issue.

template: Translate this to {{targetLanguage}}: '{{text}}'

testData.expectedOutput: Su pedido ha sido enviado y llegará en un plazo de 3 días hábiles.
testData.actualOutput: Su pedido ha sido enviado y llegará dentro de 3 días hábiles.

inputs.text: Your account has been successfully updated.

testData.expectedOutput: Su cuenta ha sido actualizada correctamente.
testData.actualOutput: Tu cuenta ha sido actualizada con éxito.

template: Write a product description for {{productName}}.

testData.expectedOutput: A concise, benefit-focused description of a portable espresso maker.
testData.actualOutput: This portable espresso maker lets you enjoy barista-quality coffee anywhere, with a compact design perfect for travel.

template: You are a copywriter for a premium {{industry}} brand. Write a 2-sentence product description for {{productName}} that emphasizes {{emphasis}}. Tone: {{tone}}.

inputs.emphasis: sound quality, comfort, and everyday versatility

testData.expectedOutput: A concise, benefit-focused description of wireless noise-canceling headphones.
testData.actualOutput: Engineered for deep focus and rich audio, these wireless headphones use advanced noise-canceling technology to silence the world around you. Lightweight and designed for all-day wear, they move seamlessly from your morning commute to late-night listening sessions.

testData.expectedOutput: A concise, benefit-focused description of a portable espresso maker.
testData.actualOutput: Wherever the day takes you, this portable espresso maker delivers a rich, cafe-quality shot in under two minutes. Compact enough for a backpack and powerful enough for the most demanding coffee enthusiast.

template: Extract all action items from this meeting note: '{{meetingNotes}}'

testData.actualOutput: 1. John: Follow up with client by Friday. 2. Sarah: Update the roadmap. 3. Team: Review new designs before Tuesday.

inputs.meetingNotes: Maria will send the revised proposal by end of day. Dev team needs to fix the login bug before the release. Everyone should complete the security training by next Monday.

testData.expectedOutput: A numbered list of action items with owner and deadline.
testData.actualOutput: 1. Maria: Send revised proposal by end of day. 2. Dev team: Fix login bug before release. 3. Everyone: Complete security training by next Monday.

inputs.meetingNotes: The design team will finalize mockups by Thursday. James needs to schedule the client demo. Finance should approve the Q3 budget before the board meeting.

testData.expectedOutput: A numbered list of action items with owner and deadline.
testData.actualOutput: 1. Design team: Finalize mockups by Thursday. 2. James: Schedule client demo. 3. Finance: Approve Q3 budget before board meeting.

inputs.meetingNotes: The design team will finalize mockups by Thursday. James needs to schedule the client demo. Finance should approve the Q3 budget before the board meeting.

template: Classify this support ticket as High, Medium, or Low priority: '{{ticketText}}'

inputs.ticketText: I cannot log into my account and have an important presentation in 2 hours.

testData.expectedOutput: High
testData.actualOutput: High

inputs.ticketText: I would like to update my billing address.

testData.expectedOutput: Low
testData.actualOutput: Low

inputs.ticketText: The dashboard is loading slowly for the past hour.

testData.expectedOutput: Medium
testData.actualOutput: Medium

inputs.ticketText: All users in our organization are locked out of the system.

testData.expectedOutput: High
testData.actualOutput: High

inputs.ticketText: Can you add a dark mode option to the settings page?

testData.expectedOutput: Low
testData.actualOutput: Low

template: You are a support triage agent. Classify the following ticket as High, Medium, or Low priority. Return JSON with fields: priority, reason. Ticket: '{{ticketText}}'

inputs.ticketText: I cannot log into my account and have an important presentation in 2 hours.

testData.expectedOutput: High
testData.actualOutput: {"priority": "High", "reason": "Login failure with a hard time constraint of 2 hours creates immediate business impact."}

inputs.ticketText: I would like to update my billing address.

testData.expectedOutput: Low
testData.actualOutput: {"priority": "Low", "reason": "Billing address update is a routine account maintenance task with no urgency."}

inputs.ticketText: The dashboard is loading slowly for the past hour.

testData.expectedOutput: Medium
testData.actualOutput: {"priority": "Medium", "reason": "Performance degradation affects usability but does not block core functionality."}

inputs.ticketText: All users in our organization are locked out of the system.

testData.expectedOutput: High
testData.actualOutput: {"priority": "High", "reason": "Organization-wide lockout represents a critical service outage with broad business impact."}

inputs.ticketText: Can you add a dark mode option to the settings page?

testData.expectedOutput: Low
testData.actualOutput: {"priority": "Low", "reason": "Feature request for a cosmetic UI enhancement with no impact on existing functionality."}

template: Rewrite this sentence to be more concise: '{{sentence}}'

testData.actualOutput: If you cannot attend the meeting, please send a substitute.

testData.expectedOutput: A concise rewrite under 12 words.
testData.actualOutput: Poor weather forced us to cancel the planned outdoor event.

inputs.sentence: In the event that you are not able to attend the meeting, please make sure to send someone in your place.

testData.expectedOutput: A concise rewrite under 12 words.

template: Identify the sentiment of this tweet: '{{tweetText}}'

inputs.tweetText: Just spent 3 hours on hold. Worst customer service I have ever experienced. Never buying from them again.

testData.expectedOutput: Negative
testData.actualOutput: Negative

inputs.tweetText: Just got my order and everything is perfect. Fast shipping and exactly as described!

testData.expectedOutput: Positive
testData.actualOutput: Positive

inputs.tweetText: Package arrived today. It's fine I guess.

testData.expectedOutput: Neutral
testData.actualOutput: Neutral

template: Analyze the sentiment of the following tweet. Return JSON with: sentiment (Positive/Neutral/Negative), confidence (0–100), and a one-sentence rationale. Tweet: '{{tweetText}}'

inputs.tweetText: Just spent 3 hours on hold. Worst customer service I have ever experienced. Never buying from them again.

testData.expectedOutput: Negative
testData.actualOutput: {"sentiment": "Negative", "confidence": 99, "rationale": "The tweet expresses strong frustration over wait time, explicitly labels the experience as the worst ever, and states intent to stop purchasing."}

inputs.tweetText: Just got my order and everything is perfect. Fast shipping and exactly as described!

testData.expectedOutput: Positive
testData.actualOutput: {"sentiment": "Positive", "confidence": 98, "rationale": "The tweet uses emphatic positive language across both delivery speed and product accuracy."}

inputs.tweetText: Package arrived today. It's fine I guess.

testData.expectedOutput: Neutral
testData.actualOutput: {"sentiment": "Neutral", "confidence": 85, "rationale": "The tweet acknowledges receipt without strong positive or negative language, suggesting mild indifference."}

template: Write a one-paragraph onboarding welcome message for a new SaaS product called {{productName}}.

template: Write a one-paragraph onboarding welcome message for a new user of {{productName}}, a {{productDescription}}. Tone: {{tone}}. Mention: {{mentions}}. Max {{maxWords}} words.

inputs.productDescription: SaaS data pipeline product targeting data engineers
inputs.mentions: getting started, key capabilities, and where to find help

testData.expectedOutput: A warm, concise onboarding message covering key capabilities and next steps.
testData.actualOutput: Welcome to DataFlow — we're glad you're here. DataFlow gives data engineers a fast, flexible way to build, monitor, and scale pipelines without the overhead. You can connect your first source in minutes using our guided setup, explore pre-built connectors, and manage transformations visually or in code. When you need help, our docs and support team are always a click away.

inputs.productDescription: SaaS project management tool targeting cross-functional teams
inputs.mentions: getting started, key capabilities, and where to find help

testData.expectedOutput: A warm, concise onboarding message covering key capabilities and next steps.
testData.actualOutput: Welcome to TaskBridge — we're excited to have you. TaskBridge gives cross-functional teams a clear, connected way to assign work, track progress, and hit deadlines without the noise. Set up your first project in minutes using our guided onboarding, explore integrations with your existing tools, and manage everything from a single dashboard. Our help center and support team are here whenever you need them.

testData.actualOutput: 1. How do I create a project? 2. Can I invite team members? 3. Is there a mobile app? 4. How do I track deadlines? 5. What integrations are available?

template: Generate {{count}} FAQs for {{productType}} targeting {{audience}}. For each FAQ, provide the question and a concise 1–2 sentence answer. Focus on {{focusAreas}}.

testData.expectedOutput: 5 relevant FAQs covering common user concerns.
testData.actualOutput: 1. Is my business data encrypted? Yes, all files are encrypted in transit and at rest using AES-256. 2. What happens if I exceed my storage limit? You will receive a notification and can upgrade your plan at any time. 3. Can multiple team members access the same files? Yes, you can invite team members and set individual permission levels. 4. What is the uptime guarantee? We offer a 99.9% uptime SLA backed by redundant infrastructure. 5. Are there long-term contracts? No, all plans are month-to-month with no cancellation fees.

inputs.focusAreas: ease of use, team collaboration, pricing, and integrations

testData.expectedOutput: 5 relevant FAQs covering common user concerns.
testData.actualOutput: 1. How quickly can my team get started? Most teams are up and running within an hour using our guided setup. 2. Can I collaborate with external contractors? Yes, you can invite guests with limited access at no extra cost. 3. Does it integrate with tools we already use? Yes, we support integrations with Slack, Google Workspace, and 50+ other tools. 4. Is there a free plan? Yes, our free plan supports up to 5 users with core features included. 5. Can I cancel anytime? Yes, all paid plans are month-to-month with no cancellation fees.

inputs.focusAreas: setup, payments, shipping, and customer management

testData.expectedOutput: 5 relevant FAQs covering common user concerns.
testData.actualOutput: 1. How long does it take to set up my store? You can have a basic store live within a day using our guided setup and pre-built templates. 2. What payment methods can I accept? We support all major credit cards, PayPal, Apple Pay, and buy-now-pay-later options. 3. How are shipping rates calculated? You can set flat rates, use real-time carrier rates, or offer free shipping with custom thresholds. 4. Can I manage returns and refunds? Yes, our built-in returns portal lets customers initiate returns and automatically updates your inventory. 5. Is customer data protected? All customer data is stored securely and fully compliant with GDPR and CCPA regulations.

template: Summarize the following article in {{count}} bullet points: '{{articleText}}'

inputs.articleText: Researchers at MIT have developed a new battery technology that charges in under 5 minutes and lasts 3x longer than lithium-ion. The technology uses a novel solid-state electrolyte and could be ready for commercial use within 5 years.

testData.expectedOutput: 3 concise bullets covering the key facts.
testData.actualOutput: - MIT developed a fast-charging battery. - It charges in under 5 minutes and lasts 3x longer than lithium-ion. - Commercial availability expected within 5 years.

inputs.articleText: A new study from Stanford found that remote workers are 13% more productive than office workers. The study tracked 500 employees over 9 months and found that remote workers took fewer sick days and reported higher job satisfaction.

testData.expectedOutput: 3 concise bullets covering the key facts.
testData.actualOutput: - Stanford study found remote workers are 13% more productive. - 500 employees tracked over 9 months. - Remote workers took fewer sick days and reported higher satisfaction.

template: Summarize the following article in exactly {{count}} bullet points for {{audience}}. Each bullet must be one sentence and highlight {{emphasis}}. Article: '{{articleText}}'

inputs.articleText: Researchers at MIT have developed a new battery technology that charges in under 5 minutes and lasts 3x longer than lithium-ion. The technology uses a novel solid-state electrolyte and could be ready for commercial use within 5 years.

testData.expectedOutput: 3 concise bullets covering the key facts.
testData.actualOutput: - MIT researchers have created a battery that charges in under 5 minutes, dramatically reducing downtime for devices and EVs. - The new solid-state technology triples the lifespan of current lithium-ion batteries, lowering long-term replacement costs. - Commercial deployment is projected within 5 years, signaling a near-term shift in the battery market.

inputs.articleText: A new study from Stanford found that remote workers are 13% more productive than office workers. The study tracked 500 employees over 9 months and found that remote workers took fewer sick days and reported higher job satisfaction.

testData.expectedOutput: 3 concise bullets covering the key facts.
testData.actualOutput: - A Stanford study of 500 employees found remote workers are 13% more productive, offering a measurable output advantage for distributed teams. - Remote workers took fewer sick days, reducing unplanned absences and associated productivity losses. - Higher reported job satisfaction among remote workers suggests long-term retention benefits for companies with flexible work policies.

template: Write a SQL query to get all users who signed up in the last {{days}} days.

testData.expectedOutput: A valid SQL query returning users signed up in the last 30 days.
testData.actualOutput: SELECT * FROM users WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days';

testData.expectedOutput: A valid SQL query returning users signed up in the last 7 days.
testData.actualOutput: SELECT * FROM users WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days';

testData.expectedOutput: A valid SQL query returning users signed up in the last 90 days.
testData.actualOutput: SELECT * FROM users WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days';

template: Write a {{dialect}} query to retrieve all users who signed up in the last {{days}} days. Return only {{fields}}. Exclude soft-deleted records where deleted_at is not null. Add a comment explaining the query.

testData.expectedOutput: A valid SQL query returning users signed up in the last 30 days.
testData.actualOutput:
-- Retrieve active users who registered within the last 30 days
SELECT id, email, created_at FROM users WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days' AND deleted_at IS NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC;

testData.expectedOutput: A valid SQL query returning users signed up in the last 7 days.
testData.actualOutput:
-- Retrieve active users who registered within the last 7 days
SELECT id, email, created_at FROM users WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days' AND deleted_at IS NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC;

testData.expectedOutput: A valid SQL query returning users signed up in the last 90 days.
testData.actualOutput:
-- Retrieve active users who registered within the last 90 days
SELECT id, email, created_at FROM users WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days' AND deleted_at IS NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC;

template: Generate {{count}} name ideas for {{appDescription}}. Each name should be short (1–2 words), memorable, and available as a .com domain. Include a one-line rationale for each.

inputs.appDescription: a B2B productivity app that helps remote teams manage async work

Cadence – Implies rhythm and structured async momentum. 4. Relay HQ – Communicates team handoffs with a sense of headquarters authority. 5. Stackpass – Suggests layered task progression and seamless passing of work.

testData.actualOutput: 1. SoloLedger – Positions the app as a personal finance tool built for independent workers. 2. Payself – Simple and direct, implying control over your own income. 3. Flowbooks – Evokes steady cash flow and clean bookkeeping. 4. Invoicly – Ties the brand to a core freelancer workflow. 5. Earnwise – Suggests financial intelligence tailored to self-employed income.

inputs.appDescription: a wellness app that tracks sleep and stress levels

testData.expectedOutput: A short, memorable app name.
testData.actualOutput: 1. Restiq – Short and modern, evoking intelligent rest tracking. 2. Calmbit – Combines calm and habit, suggesting small daily wellness actions. 3. Driftly – Implies easing into rest naturally. 4. Serenova – A softer brand evoking serenity and wellness data. 5. Napspace – Playful but direct, focused on rest and recovery.

template: Explain what {{concept}} is to a non-technical stakeholder.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the concept without jargon.
testData.actualOutput: An API is a way for two software applications to talk to each other and share information automatically.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the concept without jargon.
testData.actualOutput: A database index helps the system find information faster, like a table of contents in a book.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the concept without jargon.
testData.actualOutput: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data and improve at tasks without being explicitly programmed for each one.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the concept without jargon.
testData.actualOutput: A CDN is a network of servers that delivers web content to users from the closest location to speed up load times.

template: Explain what {{concept}} is to {{audience}} in {{sentenceLimit}} sentences or fewer. Use a real-world analogy. Avoid all technical jargon.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the concept without jargon.
testData.actualOutput: An API is like a waiter in a restaurant: you tell the waiter what you want, they take your request to the kitchen, and bring back exactly what you ordered. In software, APIs let different products and services communicate and exchange data behind the scenes. This is what allows your CRM to sync with your email tool, or your payment processor to connect with your website.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the concept without jargon.
testData.actualOutput: Think of a database index like the index in the back of a textbook — instead of flipping through every page to find a topic, you go straight to the page number listed. Without it, the system has to scan every record in the database to find what you need, which takes much longer. Adding an index means your app finds and loads information significantly faster.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the concept without jargon.
testData.actualOutput: Machine learning is like hiring an employee who gets better at their job the more experience they gain — except instead of a person, it's software learning from data. You feed it examples of past outcomes, and it figures out patterns to make predictions or decisions on new situations. This is how spam filters learn to block new junk mail, or how Netflix gets better at recommending shows you'll actually watch.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the concept without jargon.
testData.actualOutput: A CDN is like having warehouses stocked with your products in multiple cities instead of shipping everything from one location — customers get what they need from the closest warehouse, so delivery is faster. On the web, a CDN stores copies of your site's content on servers around the world so visitors load your pages from a nearby location. This means faster load times for your users no matter where they are.

template: Write a professional and empathetic {{emailType}} email. {{context}}. Keep it under {{wordLimit}} words. Do not use '{{avoidPhrase}}'.

inputs.context: The candidate interviewed but was not selected. Acknowledge their time, briefly note the decision without false praise, and leave the door open for future roles

testData.expectedOutput: A professional, empathetic decline email under 100 words.
testData.actualOutput: Hi [Name], thank you for taking the time to interview with us. After a thorough review, we've decided to move forward with another candidate whose experience more closely matches our current needs. This was a competitive process and not a reflection of your qualifications. We'll keep your information on file and encourage you to apply for future openings. Wishing you all the best.

inputs.context: The vendor submitted a proposal but was not selected. Acknowledge their effort, note the decision briefly, and keep the relationship warm for future consideration

testData.expectedOutput: A professional, empathetic decline email under 100 words.
testData.actualOutput: Hi [Name], thank you for the time and thought you put into your proposal. After careful review, we've decided to move in a different direction for this engagement. This was not an easy decision and reflects the strength of the submissions we received. We'll keep your team in mind for future opportunities and hope to find the right fit down the road.

inputs.context: The request is not a strategic fit at this time. Be respectful, avoid vague excuses, and leave the door open

testData.expectedOutput: A professional, empathetic decline email under 100 words.
testData.actualOutput: Hi [Name], thank you for thinking of us and for the detail you shared about your work. After reviewing the opportunity, we don't think it's the right fit for where we're focused right now — though that's a reflection of our current priorities, not the quality of what you're building. We'd welcome the chance to revisit this as things evolve on both sides.

template: List {{count}} best practices for {{topic}}.

testData.expectedOutput: 5 actionable best practices for the topic.
testData.actualOutput: 1. Use meaningful variable names. 2. Follow PEP 8 style guidelines. 3. Write docstrings for functions. 4. Keep functions short and focused. 5. Handle exceptions properly.

testData.expectedOutput: 5 actionable best practices for the topic.
testData.actualOutput: 1. Use semantic HTML elements. 2. Add alt text to all images. 3. Ensure sufficient color contrast. 4. Make all interactions keyboard navigable. 5. Test with a screen reader.

template: List {{count}} best practices for {{topic}} targeting {{audience}}. For each practice, include a one-line explanation and a short code example demonstrating good vs. bad usage.

testData.expectedOutput: 5 actionable best practices for the topic.
testData.actualOutput: 1. Use descriptive names: Names should reveal intent. Bad: `d = 86400` Good: `SECONDS_PER_DAY = 86400`. 2. Follow PEP 8: Consistent formatting reduces cognitive load. Bad: `def foo(x,y):return x+y` Good: `def add(x, y): return x + y`. 3. Write docstrings: Document what, not how. 4. Keep functions focused: One function, one responsibility. 5. Use specific exceptions: Bad: `except Exception` Good: `except ValueError as e`.

testData.expectedOutput: 5 actionable best practices for the topic.
testData.actualOutput:
1. Use semantic HTML: Bad: `
` Good: `

template: Describe the difference between {{conceptA}} and {{conceptB}}.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the difference between the two concepts.
testData.actualOutput: Supervised learning uses labeled data to train models to make predictions. Unsupervised learning finds patterns in data without labels.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the difference between the two concepts.
testData.actualOutput: REST uses fixed endpoints that return predefined data structures. GraphQL lets clients request exactly the data they need in a single query.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the difference between the two concepts.
testData.actualOutput: Authentication verifies who you are. Authorization determines what you are allowed to do.

template: Explain the difference between {{conceptA}} and {{conceptB}} for {{audience}}. Use one concrete example for each. Keep the total response under {{wordLimit}} words.

inputs.audience: a product manager with no ML background

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the difference between the two concepts.
testData.actualOutput: Supervised learning trains a model on labeled examples so it can predict outcomes on new data — like training a spam filter on emails already marked spam or not spam. Unsupervised learning finds hidden patterns in unlabeled data — like grouping customers by purchase behavior without telling the model what the groups should be. The key difference is whether you're teaching the model the right answer upfront.

inputs.audience: a product manager with no engineering background

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the difference between the two concepts.
testData.actualOutput: REST is like ordering from a fixed menu — you request a specific dish and get exactly what's listed, even if you only wanted part of it. GraphQL is like telling the kitchen exactly what you want — just the protein, no sides — and getting back only that. REST is simpler to set up; GraphQL is more efficient when different clients need different data from the same source.

testData.expectedOutput: A plain-language explanation of the difference between the two concepts.
testData.actualOutput: Authentication is the process of confirming who you are — like showing your ID at the door. Authorization is what happens next: determining what you're allowed to do once you're inside — like whether you can access the VIP section. In software, authentication verifies your login, and authorization controls which features, data, or actions you have access to based on your role.

template: Generate a regex pattern that validates {{validationTarget}}.

testData.expectedOutput: A valid regex pattern for the target format.
A valid regex pattern for the target format.
A valid regex pattern for the target format.

template: Generate a {{language}} regex pattern that validates {{validationTarget}} including formats: {{formats}}. Include a brief explanation of each part of the pattern and a test snippet.

testData.expectedOutput: A valid regex pattern for the target format.
testData.actualOutput: Pattern: /^(\+1\s?)?\(?\d{3}\)?[\s.\-]?\d{3}[\s.\-]?\d{4}$/. Breakdown: optional country code, area code with optional parens, optional separator, 7-digit local number. Test: phoneRegex.test('(555) 555-5555') // true

testData.expectedOutput: A valid regex pattern for the target format.
testData.actualOutput: Pattern: /^\d{5}(-\d{4})?$/. Breakdown: 5 required digits, optional hyphen + 4-digit extension. Test: zipRegex.test('12345') // true, zipRegex.test('12345-6789') // true

testData.expectedOutput: A valid regex pattern for the target format.
testData.actualOutput: Pattern: /^[\w.+\-]+@[\w\-]+(?:\.[\w\-]+)*\.[a-zA-Z]{2,}$/. Breakdown: local part allows word chars, dots, plus, hyphen; domain segments separated by dots; TLD 2+ chars. Test: emailRegex.test('user@example.com') // true

template: Summarize the key benefits of {{topic}} for {{audience}}.

testData.expectedOutput: A concise summary of key benefits without process jargon.
testData.actualOutput: Agile helps teams work faster by breaking projects into smaller pieces, getting frequent feedback, and adapting to changes quickly.

testData.expectedOutput: A concise summary of key benefits without process jargon.
testData.actualOutput: Cloud computing lets businesses access software and storage over the internet, reducing the need for expensive hardware and enabling teams to work from anywhere.

testData.expectedOutput: A concise summary of key benefits without process jargon.
testData.actualOutput: Automated testing helps catch bugs earlier, speeds up releases, and reduces the risk of shipping broken features to customers.

template: Summarize the top {{count}} business benefits of {{topic}} for {{audience}} in {{count}} bullet points. Each bullet should be one sentence and focus on {{emphasis}}.

testData.expectedOutput: A concise summary of key benefits without process jargon.
testData.actualOutput: - Agile reduces time-to-market by delivering working increments of a product on a regular cadence. - It lowers risk by surfacing problems early through continuous feedback loops. - Teams can respond to shifting business priorities mid-project without costly rework.

testData.expectedOutput: A concise summary of key benefits without process jargon.
testData.actualOutput: - Cloud computing eliminates large upfront infrastructure costs by shifting to a predictable monthly operating expense. - It enables teams to scale capacity up or down instantly based on demand, reducing waste and over-provisioning. - Remote access to all tools and data supports distributed teams without relying on on-site hardware.

testData.expectedOutput: A concise summary of key benefits without process jargon.
testData.actualOutput: - Automated testing reduces the cost of bugs by catching them before they reach customers, when fixes are cheapest. - It enables faster release cycles by removing the bottleneck of manual QA on every update. - Consistent test coverage lowers the risk of regressions, protecting product reliability as the codebase grows.
